quickAnswer.heading: Quick answer
items: For weed control, the strongest landscape fabric is heavy-duty woven polypropylene, UV-stabilized and permeable
Look for 3.2oz per square yard or heavier for long-term beds and mulch
Woven blocks light without pre-punched holes; avoid light perforated fabric
Quality fabric lasts 10+ years under mulch or gravel
SHIELD for beds and weed control, XBAR for gravel and load, TERRA for drainage

blocks.heading: Landscape fabric for weed control, the 2026 buyer's guide
blocks.body: Weeds cost gardeners an estimated 70 hours of labor a year, so it is no wonder so many people search for a smarter solution before buying. But with woven, non-woven, geotextile, and plastic sheeting all on the market, choosing the wrong one is an expensive mistake. This guide answers the questions that decide which landscape fabric for weed control actually fits your project, with straight answers and no fluff. Not sure which fits? Use the [DOTDAY Fabric Finder](https://www.thedotday.com/fabric-finder) or size your order with the [Fabric Calculator](https://www.thedotday.com/landscape-fabric-calculator).

blocks.heading: What landscape fabric is and how it works
blocks.body: Landscape fabric, also called weed barrier fabric or ground cover, is a permeable textile laid on soil to block weeds without chemicals. It cuts off the sunlight weed seeds need to germinate while staying permeable enough to let water, air, and nutrients pass to the root zone. Unlike solid black plastic sheeting, which suffocates soil and harms beneficial organisms, a quality woven fabric like [SHIELD](https://www.thedotday.com/product-page/dotday-shield-weed-barrier-fabric-heavy-duty-3-2oz-landscape-fabric) keeps the soil ecosystem healthy and breathing. That is the difference between a bed that thrives and one that slowly deteriorates under plastic.

blocks.heading: What to look for in weed-control fabric
rows: Blocks light by weave, not pre-punched holes weeds push through
Heavier resists tearing and lasts longer under cover
Keeps soil healthy and prevents pooling
Survives sun exposure at edges instead of going brittle

blocks.alt: Planting beds before and after landscape fabric for weed control with DOTDAY SHIELD
blocks.caption: A correctly matched, fully covered woven barrier is what cuts the weeding down to almost nothing.

blocks.heading: Does landscape fabric actually work?
blocks.body: Yes, when quality and install are right. A premium woven fabric dramatically reduces weed emergence compared to bare soil, while light perforated fabric fails fast because weeds push through its holes and debris roots on top. The fabric is only half of it: full coverage, generous 6 to 12 inch overlaps, and keeping the surface clean are what make it last. For the honest version of where fabric helps and where it does not, read [do weed barriers actually work](https://www.thedotday.com/post/do-weed-barriers-actually-work).

blocks.heading: Buying weed-control fabric for a crew or farm?
blocks.body: DOTDAY supplies SHIELD to landscapers, growers, and nurseries direct. Request bulk and pro pricing for multi-site coverage.

blocks.heading: Which DOTDAY fabric is right for your project?
blocks.body: Match the fabric to the ground condition. [SHIELD](https://www.thedotday.com/product-page/dotday-shield-weed-barrier-fabric-heavy-duty-3-2oz-landscape-fabric) 3.2oz woven is the weed-control workhorse for beds, mulch, farms, and nurseries. [XBAR](https://www.thedotday.com/product-page/dotday-xbar-weed-barrier-fabric-heavy-duty-5oz-dual-layer) 5oz dual-layer is for gravel, pavers, driveways, and high-traffic ground where the fabric carries load. [TERRA](https://www.thedotday.com/product-page/dotday-terra-geotextile-fabric-4-oz-non-woven-landscape-fabric) non-woven geotextile is for drainage, French drains, and soil separation. To compare durability across weights, read [what landscape fabric lasts the longest](https://www.thedotday.com/post/what-landscape-fabric-lasts-the-longest), and for weight specifics see [3.2oz vs 5oz weed barrier](https://www.thedotday.com/post/3-2oz-vs-5oz-weed-barrier).

blocks.heading: How much do you need?
blocks.body: Order enough fabric to cover the area plus overlap. The [DOTDAY Fabric Calculator](https://www.thedotday.com/landscape-fabric-calculator) takes your dimensions and returns the rolls to order with overlap built in, and the [Fabric Finder](https://www.thedotday.com/fabric-finder) matches your project type and ground condition to the right fabric. Buy the right weight once and you avoid years of re-doing the job.

blocks.heading: Find the right fabric in two minutes
blocks.body: Answer a few questions about your ground condition and get matched to SHIELD, XBAR, or TERRA for the job.

items.q: What landscape fabric works best for weed control?
items.a: Heavy-duty woven polypropylene that is UV-stabilized and permeable to water and air, rated 3.2oz per square yard or heavier. Woven construction blocks the light weeds need without the pre-punched holes that let weeds through light perforated fabric. DOTDAY SHIELD meets these criteria for beds and mulch, while XBAR steps up to 5oz for gravel and high-traffic ground where the fabric also carries load.

items.q: Does landscape fabric actually work for weed control?
items.a: Yes, when the fabric is quality and the install is right. A premium woven barrier dramatically cuts weed emergence versus bare soil by blocking sunlight from below. Failures usually come from light perforated fabric, skipped overlaps, sun exposure, or debris rooting on top. Pair the right woven fabric with full coverage, 6 to 12 inch overlaps, and a clean surface and it performs for years.

items.q: How long does landscape fabric last?
items.a: A quality UV-stabilized woven fabric lasts well over 10 years under mulch or gravel, while light perforated fabric often fails within a couple of seasons. Lifespan depends heavily on keeping the fabric covered from sunlight and matching weight to load. Woven fabric like SHIELD and XBAR sits at the long end of that range when installed and covered correctly.
